entry.title: Week 18, 2026: OCaml 5.5 Wasm support completed, several compiler and runtime bug fixes merged, QuickJS-NG support proposed.
entry.content:
Summary

New PRs target QuickJS-NG support, DOM API updates, OCaml 5.6, and older-browser docs; two tracking issues closed.

Activity

  • QuickJS-NG support proposed by @hhugo in #2229: adds a quickjs dune profile with tools/quickjs_wrapper.exe, a runtime/js/quickjs_shim.js compatibility shim filling in Node-only globals, fixes to host-portability bugs surfaced by QuickJS, and a make tests-quickjs CI target; stacked on #2216 and #2228
  • DOM API type corrections opened by Jérôme Vouillon in #2221 (3215 additions, 91 deletions): fixes Dom_html.inputElement.defaultChecked type from js_string t prop to bool t prop, corrects Dom_html.createVideo return type from audioElement t to videoElement t, and fixes Dom_html.selection.collapse signature to match the actual JS Selection.collapse(node, offset?) API; addresses #679 and #453
  • Initial OCaml 5.6 support draft opened by Jérôme Vouillon in #2227 (286 additions across 17 files)
  • Oxcaml 5.2.0minus31 update draft opened by Jérôme Vouillon in #2225, with @hhugo raising a question about backward compatibility with the previous OxCaml version
  • Browser compatibility documentation (new manual/browser-compat.wiki chapter on downleveling with Babel and core-js) remains open in #2216 by @hhugo; also fixes ECMASCRIPT.md (async/await are ES2017, not ES2016) and adds Promise and template literals
  • OCaml 5.5 support tracking issue #2193 closed after @hhugo confirmed master supports OCaml 5.5; Kate noted the 5.5 release candidate is expected by mid-May
  • Wasm testing tracking issue #2061 closed after upstream dependencies were resolved
  • dune.inc cleanup merged by @hhugo in #2222, removing 234 lines through rule consolidation
  • OxCaml CI workflow fix merged by Jérôme Vouillon in #2219
  • Long-running draft PRs remain active: physical equality hints (#1745), OCaml upstream hints consumption (#1721), compiler substitution speedup (#2185), and OCaml 5.5 full compiler+runtime support (#2195, currently conflicting)
  • Intl.RelativeTimeFormat bindings remain open from @toastal in #2070
entry.summary: OCaml 5.5 Wasm support completed, several compiler and runtime bug fixes merged, QuickJS-NG support proposed.

entry.title: Week 17, 2026: Browser compatibility discussion prompted a new Babel documentation PR; CI updated with actions/checkout v6.
entry.content:
Summary

New documentation PR for Babel-based older browser targeting opened; dependabot bumped CI checkout action.

Activity

  • @hhugo opened #2216 — a new manual chapter documenting how to target older browsers using Babel and core-js to downlevel jsofocaml's ES2020 output, with an end-to-end cram test (compiler/tests-jsoo/babel-downlevel/) gated behind a @runtest-babel-downlevel alias so it stays out of the default @runtest; the PR also fixes ECMASCRIPT.md (async/await are ES2017, not ES2016) and adds Promise and template literals (ES2015) to the feature table. This PR is open and awaiting review.
  • Dependabot merged #2214, bumping actions/checkout from v5 to v6 in the CI workflow.
  • The long-running draft PR #833 — an alternative interface for jsooTop (JsooTopWrapped, JsooTopAsynchronous) originally developed for the OCaml MOOC — received an update to its metadata this week; it remains a WIP draft.
entry.summary: Browser compatibility discussion prompted a new Babel documentation PR; CI updated with actions/checkout v6.

entry.title: Week 16, 2026: OxCaml support merged (140 files, Float32/int8/int16/null types) alongside JSPI documentation clarifications.
entry.content:
Summary

Two open Wasm optimization PRs and two blocked Wasm feature PRs remain active alongside a long-standing jsooTop WIP draft.

Activity

  • Wasm toplevel function size reduction is being pursued via two open PRs by Jérôme Vouillon: #2211 puts constant expressions at toplevel into global variables (halving the PRT benchmark toplevel function size), and draft #2212 extracts loops from the toplevel function into helper functions so that Wasm engine tiering (Turbofan/V8 optimization) only applies to small helpers rather than the large, once-called init function.
  • WASI runtime (#1831) and Wasm native effects via Stack Switching (#2189) remain blocked on #2177, ocaml/dune#13613, and a new dune release. The WASI PR supports --enable wasi and --enable exnref flags for producing Wasm binaries runnable with Wizard or wasmtime.
  • Alternative jsooTop interface (#833) remains open as a long-standing WIP draft, providing JsooTopWrapped with first-class error/warning values and an Lwt-based JsooTopAsynchronous implementation.
entry.summary: OxCaml support merged (140 files, Float32/int8/int16/null types) alongside JSPI documentation clarifications.

entry.title: Week 15, 2026: Wasm dynlink and toplevel support landed, with two follow-up Wasm size-reduction PRs opened.
entry.content:
Summary

Two new Wasm size-reduction PRs opened following the dynlink/toplevel merge, both targeting toplevel function overhead.

Activity

  • Two new Wasm optimization PRs opened by Jérôme Vouillon as follow-up work after the dynlink/toplevel merge: #2211 puts constant expressions into global variables to reduce toplevel function size (halving it for the PRT benchmark), and #2212 (draft) extracts loops from the toplevel function into helper functions to avoid wasteful Wasm engine tier-up of the large init function
  • Dynlink / toplevel support (#2187) merged after development since February, bringing 6383 additions across 139 files including predefined exception disambiguation in caml_register_global, Global_name module extraction, and link_info refactoring
entry.summary: Wasm dynlink and toplevel support landed, with two follow-up Wasm size-reduction PRs opened.

entry.title: Week 14, 2026: Three compiler and Wasm bug fixes merged by Jérôme Vouillon, addressing reference unboxing and Wasm direct call issues.
entry.content:
Summary

Three bug fix PRs merged; open work includes Dynlink/toplevel Wasm support, compiler speedup, OCaml 5.5 support, and ESM tooling.

Activity

  • Jérôme Vouillon continues work on Dynlink and toplevel support for the Wasm backend, a large in-progress PR with over 6000 lines of changes (#2187)
  • @hhugo has an open PR for compiler substitution speedup aimed at reducing compile times (#2185), and a draft PR for initial OCaml 5.5 runtime and compiler support with extensive changes across 73 files (#2195)
  • @hhugo also has a draft PR for initial ECMAScript modules (ESM) tooling in the JS compiler backend (#2163)
  • @hhugo closed the long-running draft PR experimenting with an internal API change without merging (#2019)
  • Jérôme Vouillon merged three Wasm and compiler bug fixes this week: reference unboxing fix (#2210), empty closure identity fix (#2207), and stale data flow guard (#2208)
entry.summary: Three compiler and Wasm bug fixes merged by Jérôme Vouillon, addressing reference unboxing and Wasm direct call issues.

entry.title: Week 13, 2026: CI tooling maintenance week with Biome linter update and two new Wasm correctness fixes opened.
entry.content:
Summary

Two new Wasm correctness PRs opened; ongoing large PRs for Dynlink support and OCaml 5.5 compatibility remain active.

Activity

  • Jérôme Vouillon opened #2207 to fix Wasm empty closure identity: empty closures were all physically equal due to sharing a (ref.i31 (i32.const 0)) placeholder, breaking (==) comparisons between distinct closures; the fix allocates a distinct global struct.new for each empty closure
  • Jérôme Vouillon opened #2208 to guard Wasm direct calls against stale global data flow data, preventing miscompilation when Global_flow.get_unique_closure returns a closure variable that was subsequently eliminated by dead code analysis
  • The large Dynlink / toplevel support PR #2187 by Jérôme Vouillon received activity from @hhugo, who contributed additions and requested review; the PR touches 140 files with 6362 additions
  • The draft OCaml 5.5 initial support PR #2195 by @hhugo (72 files, 11865 additions) was updated, remaining in draft state
  • @hhugo updated Biome to 2.4.8 and fixed associated lint warnings across the JS runtime and configuration files (#2202)
  • A series of Windows CI fixes were attempted and then reverted: dune was pinned to 3.21.0 to work around a cram test regression in 3.22.0 where /cygdrive/ paths caused all commands to output "UNREACHABLE" (#2203), but the fix was ineffective and reverted (#2204); similarly, setup-ocaml was pinned to v3.4.8 (#2205) and then reverted (#2206)
entry.summary: CI tooling maintenance week with Biome linter update and two new Wasm correctness fixes opened.

entry.title: Week 12, 2026: Variable coalescing and Wasm bigstring optimizations merged, reducing JS register usage by up to 91% on large programs.
entry.content:
Summary

Active development on Wasm features (dynlink, native effects, WASI) and compiler improvements (OCaml 5.5, OxCaml, subst speedup).

Activity

  • Ongoing Wasm dynlink/toplevel unification work in PR #2187 by Jérôme Vouillon, with @hhugo adding commits to align Wasm and JS toplevel behavior; review discussion active this week on exception registration, hash tables, and binary search tradeoffs
  • OCaml 5.5 initial support in draft PR #2195 by @hhugo with 12,000+ lines of changes across 89 files, currently blocked by merge conflicts
  • OxCaml support in long-running PR #2105 by Jérôme Vouillon saw review activity this week from @hhugo covering CI matrix conditions, ocamlformat compatibility, and attribute-based conditional compilation
  • Wasm native effects via the Stack Switching proposal in PR #2189 by Jérôme Vouillon, blocked on #2177 and ocaml/dune#13613; discussion this week noted that Node 26 removed --experimental-wasm-stack-switching (now always enabled) and proposed documenting per-node feature flags
  • WASI runtime in PR #1831 by Jérôme Vouillon, supporting --enable wasi and --enable exnref flags for producing binaries runnable by Wizard and wasmtime engines, currently has merge conflicts
  • Compiler subst speedup in PR #2185 by @hhugo and block sharing preservation in PR #2184 both awaiting merge
  • Variable coalescing assertion fix for empty try bodies merged (#2201) and inline duplication fix for inline_recursively path merged (#2200), both addressing edge cases exposed by the new coalescing and arity-tracking work from #2198
entry.summary: Variable coalescing and Wasm bigstring optimizations merged, reducing JS register usage by up to 91% on large programs.

entry.title: Week 11, 2026: New --build-config CLI flags, OCaml 5.5 alpha support, shape computation performance fix, and runtime fixes merged.
entry.content:
Summary

Ongoing Wasm backend work continues on dynlink/toplevel support, WASI runtime, and ECMAScript modules; OxCaml support PR remains under review.

Activity

  • Ongoing: Dynlink and toplevel support for Wasm (#2187) by Jérôme Vouillon is open and under review, with @hhugo providing detailed feedback on the read-only embedded filesystem behavior (chdir, mkdir, stat) and code sharing opportunities.
  • Ongoing: WASI runtime (#1831) by Jérôme Vouillon remains blocked on #2177 (now merged), ocaml/dune#13613, and #2189.
  • Draft: OCaml 5.5 full support (#2195) by @hhugo is a large draft PR (12k additions) covering comprehensive Wasm and JS changes for OCaml 5.5, with a known Wasm int test failure under investigation by Jérôme Vouillon.
  • Ongoing: OxCaml support (#2105) by Jérôme Vouillon is under continued review by @hhugo, covering or_null type support via JavaScript null and ppxlib integration.
  • Draft: ECMAScript modules tooling (#2163) by @hhugo remains in draft with initial compiler tooling for ESM output.
  • Draft: Compiler hints work by @hhugo spans two related draft PRs: consuming OCaml physical equality hints (#1745) and hints produced by ocaml/ocaml#13438 (#1721).
  • onbeforeunload handler fix (#2159) by @hhugo fixing #1436 remains open, adding Dom.beforeUnloadEvent and Dom_html.beforeUnloadEvent class types and correcting the boolean return value handling for modern browsers.
  • Issue closed: Cedric Cellier reported a build failure with wasm-merge: not found when installing wasm_of_ocaml-compiler 6.3.2 (#2190), resolved by the Binaryen version check added in #2191.
entry.summary: New --build-config CLI flags, OCaml 5.5 alpha support, shape computation performance fix, and runtime fixes merged.

entry.title: Week 10, 2026: OCaml 5.5 support work initiated with issue and draft PR, plus bug fix PRs nearing completion
entry.content:
Summary

OCaml 5.5 support work opened, runtime and compiler bug fixes prepared for merge

Activity

  • OCaml 5.5 support work initiated with Kate opening issue requesting release availability (#2193), followed by @hhugo creating draft PR for initial compiler and runtime support with 11,849 additions and 6,991 deletions across 73 files (#2195)
  • Runtime fixes PR by Jérôme Vouillon addressing wasm runtime issues including float parsing, MD5 buffer offsets, and JavaScript array conversion, prepared for merge (#2192)
  • Bug fixes PR by Jérôme Vouillon correcting global flow hash functions, evaluation optimizations, and wasm linker import descriptors, ready for merge (#2194)
  • Wasm runtime enhancement checking for Binaryen >= 119 availability completed by Jérôme Vouillon, marked with "no changelog" label (#2191)
  • Unboxing optimization work for function and block parameters continues as long-running draft PR from April 2025 by Jérôme Vouillon with 1,066 additions (#1953)
entry.summary: OCaml 5.5 support work initiated with issue and draft PR, plus bug fix PRs nearing completion

entry.title: Week 9, 2026: Development focused on Wasm runtime improvements with bug reports, version checks, and runtime fixes
entry.content:
Summary

Two new PRs opened for runtime fixes and Binaryen version checking, with ongoing Wasm-related development

Activity

  • Jérôme Vouillon opened runtime fixes PR addressing multiple issues across 13 files (#2192) and Binaryen version check PR to ensure version >= 119 is available for Wasm builds (#2191)
  • Wasm native effects implementation using Stack Switching proposal continues development with 414 additions across 31 files by Jérôme Vouillon (#2189)
  • Build configuration flags PR introducing --build-config and --apply-build-config for coherent compilation settings across linked units by Jérôme Vouillon (#2177)
  • Dynlink and toplevel support for Wasm continues with 3602 additions across 71 files by Jérôme Vouillon (#2187)
  • WASI runtime implementation remains under development with 5260 additions across 83 files, marked as blocked (#1831)
  • Two PRs closed without merging: benchmark comparison test (#2099) and safepoint table workaround (#2165)
entry.summary: Development focused on Wasm runtime improvements with bug reports, version checks, and runtime fixes

entry.title: Week 8, 2026: Wasm performance optimizations merged, dynlink/toplevel support and native effects PRs opened, CI updated to OCaml 5.4.0
entry.content:
Summary

Two new Wasm PRs opened (dynlink/toplevel support, native effects), three PRs merged (string optimizations, Wasm fixes, CI update)

Activity

  • Dynlink and toplevel support PR opened by Jérôme Vouillon with 3601 additions across 71 files, enabling virtual filesystem support for toplevel examples like lwt_toplevel (#2187)
  • Wasm native effects PR opened by Jérôme Vouillon adding 369 lines across 31 files for improved effect handling (#2189)
  • String conversion and arraybuffer optimizations merged by Jérôme Vouillon after extended development period, delivering faster Wasm runtime performance (#2124)
  • Wasm fixes PR merged addressing multiple runtime issues across 9 files (#2154)
  • CI updated to install OCaml 5.4.0 for testing compatibility with latest compiler (#2188)
  • Variable coalescing optimization PR under review with measurements showing 74-91% register reduction and 10-16% compressed size reduction, particularly effective when combined with constant sinking (#2166)
  • Build configuration flags PR in review introducing --build-config and --apply-build-config for coherent multi-unit compilation (#2177)
  • Safepoint table workaround PR discussions continued with input from @jakobkummerow noting that Chrome 147+ improvements may eliminate need for the workaround (#2165)
  • OxCaml support PR under active review with @hhugo providing feedback on alert handling and optimization differences (#2105)
  • ECMAScript modules tooling PR remains in draft status (#2163)
entry.summary: Wasm performance optimizations merged, dynlink/toplevel support and native effects PRs opened, CI updated to OCaml 5.4.0

entry.title: Week 7, 2026: Two patch releases (6.3.1, 6.3.2) shipped to fix shell completion install, with compiler performance and internal API improvements.
entry.content:
Summary

Active week of compiler performance work merged and two patch releases shipped to fix shell completion installation regressions.

Activity

  • Newly opened and still-open PRs include #2185 (Compiler: speedup subst by @hhugo), #2184 (Preserve sharing of blocks by @hhugo), and #2186 (Fix auto complete install again by @hhugo) which was quickly merged this week
  • Compiler performance improvements landed across several merged PRs: BitSet iteration speedup (#2179), Set.cardinal avoidance (#2180), and Set shortcut operations (#2182) by @hhugo
  • Compiler cleanup batch of small refactors merged (#2178) by @hhugo, covering Map.update adoption, find_opt usage, and removal of unnecessary modules
  • Two patch releases shipped: 6.3.1 (2026-02-13) and 6.3.2 (2026-02-15) both addressing shell completion file installation issues in monorepo builds, triggered by a bug report from Nicolás Ojeda Bär about workspace build failures (#2181 closed without merge, superseded by #2186)
  • Long-running open PRs continue to accumulate review discussion: #2166 (Variable coalescing by Jérôme Vouillon) saw an assertion failure report from @hhugo during rebase; #2159 (onbeforeunload fix by @hhugo) awaits review from Jérôme Vouillon
  • Wasm-related PRs remain open in draft or conflicting state: #2154 (Wasm fixes), #2144 (Bigstring optimizations), #1831 (WASI runtime), and #1832 (Stack Switching effects), all by Jérôme Vouillon
  • OXCaml support PR #2105 by Jérôme Vouillon received design questions from @hhugo around Null representation compatibility with #949
entry.summary: Two patch releases (6.3.1, 6.3.2) shipped to fix shell completion install, with compiler performance and internal API improvements.

entry.title: Week 6, 2026: Release 6.3.0 with constant sinking optimization, improved DOM bindings, and compiler bug fixes
entry.content:
Summary

Finalized 6.3.0 release with 67 commits improving compiler correctness, DOM bindings, and examples

Activity

  • Version 6.3.0 released on February 6, 2026 by @hhugo with 121 downloads of the release tarball, containing compilation optimizations, DOM binding fixes, and enhanced examples (release 6.3.0)
  • Compiler optimization work included new constant sinking pass by Jérôme Vouillon (2d01350), elimination of unnecessary boolean conversions (8627d6f), and improved generated name tracking (e11a80e, 4bf89f0)
  • Compiler correctness fixes addressed parallel renaming bug (9cf9411), Globalflow monotonicity (61b2c25), static evaluation of camlnativeinttoint (51abfcd), invalid simplification (2b6f607), and targetint bounds (80ccb02)
  • DOM binding improvements by @hhugo fixed numerous type issues in Domhtml (submitEvent, mediaQueryListEvent, pointerEvent, element scrollWidth/scrollHeight), Domsvg (numberList), and other modules (eventSource.url, file types, geolocation timestamp, WebGL constants), plus removed non-existent Js.date methods (2b57432, c8d6e12, 5798832, e23569f)
  • Example and documentation enhancements included 12 commits improving demo applications (boulderdash, cubes, graph viewer, graphics, hyperbolic, minesweeper, planet, webgl, wiki, wysiwyg), toplevel examples with better graphics support, and new API status documentation with improved manual organization (multiple commits)
  • Runtime improvements fixed graphics resize/clear functionality (238d361) and Windows build compatibility (13dfd19)
  • Testing infrastructure added new tests for scopes, forIn/forOf loops (2ed2277), constant sinking behavior, and bool context handling, plus reorganized test directories by moving namespace and separate compilation examples out of examples/ (7af7c03, c3f4a92)
entry.summary: Release 6.3.0 with constant sinking optimization, improved DOM bindings, and compiler bug fixes

entry.title: Week 5, 2026: JavaScript parser improvements, runtime eval enhancements, comprehensive manual rewrite, and loop miscompilation fix merged
entry.content:
Summary

Three PRs opened for static evaluation experiments, 6.3 release preparation, and static evaluation; wasm runtime and manual documentation work completed

Activity

  • Static evaluation experiment PR opened by Jérôme Vouillon exploring compile-time optimization opportunities (#2153)
  • Release preparation for version 6.3 opened by @hhugo addressing pending PRs and runtime value re-export (#2152)
  • Wasm runtime eval functionality merged providing JavaScript eval function access in WebAssembly runtime after review discussions (#2108)
  • Manual documentation underwent substantial rewrite adding quickstart guide, consolidated JavaScript interop documentation, error handling guide, syntax reference, improved options and PPX documentation, plus new runtime representation guide (#2150)
  • JavaScript parser test infrastructure enhanced with tc39/test262 integration, test flexibility improvements, and recovery strategy enhancements (#2149)
  • Popover API implementation continues with review addressing property capitalization, options handling, and specification updates by Sylvain Boilard (#1734)
  • Ongoing draft work includes JavaScript string implementation for Wasm (#1772), function/block parameter unboxing (#1953), primitive inlining (#1928), exception handling via null returns (#1910), Stack Switching proposal effects (#1832), WASI runtime (#1831), and Oxcaml support (#2105)
  • Performance optimizations awaiting review include aggressive lambda lifting (#1886), bigstring operations (#2144), string/arraybuffer conversions (#2124), and exception handling (#2098)
  • Library enhancements in progress include CSS Font Loading API wrapper by Mesmera (WAFT) (#1833) and Intl.RelativeTimeFormat API by @toastal (#2070)
  • Compiler infrastructure work includes OCaml hints consumption from ocaml/ocaml#13438 (#1721), lambda lifting refinements (#1885), and Wasm type precision improvements (#1907, #1908)
entry.summary: JavaScript parser improvements, runtime eval enhancements, comprehensive manual rewrite, and loop miscompilation fix merged

entry.title: Week 4, 2026: JavaScript parser modernization with ES6+ features including using declarations, import attributes, and defer imports
entry.content:
Summary

Parser modernization and testing with two new PRs opened and ongoing WebAssembly optimization work

Activity

  • Parser improvements continue with @hhugo opening new PR for further enhancements (#2149) following up on week's extensive parser refactoring
  • Merged work includes JavaScript parser modernization aligned with ECMAScript spec (#2143), contextual keyword handling without parser size explosion (#2148), import defer support (#2147), and license file typo fix (#2145)
  • WebAssembly optimizations actively developed by Jérôme Vouillon with bigstring operations PR showing 2-4x performance improvements in Firefox and Chromium (#2144), string conversion optimizations (#2124), and ongoing WASI runtime implementation (#1831)
  • Oxcaml support continues under development as draft PR with over 11,000 additions affecting 144 files (#2105)
entry.summary: JavaScript parser modernization with ES6+ features including using declarations, import attributes, and defer imports

entry.title: Week 3, 2026: Shell completion installation, Wasm runtime marshal fix, and JavaScript parser issue opened
entry.content:
Summary

JavaScript parser modernization PR opened, trunk support issue created, and number type conversion discussion

Activity

  • JavaScript parser modernization PR opened by @hhugo to align the parser with ECMAScript spec and fix arrow function parsing in for loop definitions (#2143, fixes #2139)
  • OCaml trunk support enhancement issue opened by @hhugo to track compatibility with upcoming OCaml compiler changes (#2142, references ocaml/ocaml#14469)
  • Number type conversion discussion closed after @tari3x sought guidance on updating code affected by new number type changes from #1650 (#2136)
  • Dune workaround implemented to address issue dune#13307 when installing multiple share_root targets with cmdliner completion scripts (4e91eec)
entry.summary: Shell completion installation, Wasm runtime marshal fix, and JavaScript parser issue opened

entry.title: Week 52, 2025: Parser bug report for arrow functions in for loops and ongoing effects-based Stack Switching implementation
entry.content:
Summary

Parser bug identified and ongoing WebAssembly effects implementation continues

Activity

  • JavaScript parser bug reported by Spotandjake where arrow functions in for loop variable definitions fail to parse, discovered while integrating libbinaryen ES6 package (#2139)
  • WebAssembly effects implementation based on Stack Switching proposal continues as draft PR by Jérôme Vouillon, requiring Binaryen 124+ and producing Wasm code executable by Wizard engine with stack-switching extension (#1832)

entry.title: Week 51, 2025: Discussion activity on WASM stack switching and number type conversion issues
entry.content:
Summary

Ongoing discussion on WASM effects implementation and Binaryen dependency updates

Activity

  • WASM stack switching development continued with Linxuan M from the Wizard engine team asking about Binaryen dependency management and Jérôme Vouillon clarifying that the outdated binaryen-bin dependency issue was resolved in #2134 (#1832)
  • Number type conversion issue discussed by @tari3x regarding migration challenges introduced by changes from #1650, seeking advice on upgrading code that no longer compiles (#2136)

entry.title: Week 50, 2025: Dependency management improvements with Binaryen documentation updates and issue discussions on compatibility and licensing
entry.content:
Summary

Merged Binaryen dependency improvements and ongoing discussions on stack overflow issues, WASI runtime, and API migration challenges

Activity

  • Binaryen dependency management improved by @hhugo through merging #2134, which switched to conf-binaryen package enabling use of distribution-provided Binaryen tools instead of building from source, addressing #2131 by @shym
  • WASI runtime development continues with Jérôme Vouillon discussing merge timeline for #1831, while #2108 remains open for JavaScript eval function access in Wasm runtime
  • Draft benchmark PR #2099 reviewed by @hhugo questioning whether still needed, with 17K additions and 24K deletions affecting 326 files
  • Opam repository archival PR #2138 closed without merging after being opened by @hhugo

entry.title: Week 49, 2025: Runtime improvements and documentation cleanup with new API for accessing JavaScript runtime values
entry.content:
Summary

Runtime improvements for Deno support and binaryen dependency work ongoing

Activity

  • Jérôme Vouillon has Wasm runtime work in progress providing JavaScript eval function access for Wasm runtime with merge conflicts to resolve (#2108)

entry.content:

Runtime improvements opened for Deno support and CI workflow dependency update merged

  • Dependabot updated GitHub Actions workflows by bumping actions/checkout from version 5 to 6, merged by maintainers (#2130)

entry.title: Week 46, 2025: Browser target bug fix merged, ongoing WebAssembly development continues
entry.content:
Summary

WebAssembly eval function and benchmark PRs remain in development

Activity

  • Jérôme Vouillon's WebAssembly runtime PR providing access to JavaScript eval function continues in review with pending comments from @hhugo before merge (#2108)

entry.title: Week 45, 2025: PPX bug fixes for unused self warnings and labelled arguments in object methods merged this week
entry.content:
Summary

Development focused on effects implementation, WASI runtime, and Oxcaml support alongside PPX maintenance work

Activity

  • @hhugo merged PPX fix for unused self variable warnings in object%js (#2128)
  • Jérôme Vouillon merged ppx_optcomp_light improvements for conditional module inclusion and attribute handling (#2117) and PPX cleanup work (#2120)
  • Draft PR for effects implementation based on Stack Switching proposal continues development, targeting Wasm code generation with Binaryen and Wizard engine support (#1832)
  • WASI runtime PR remains open with support for producing WASI binaries using --enable wasi flag, executable with both Wizard engine and wasmtime (#1831)
  • Draft PR for Oxcaml support continues with over 10,000 lines of additions across 147 files (#2105)
  • Jérôme Vouillon opened draft PR for string conversion and arraybuffer optimizations targeting Wasm improvements (#2124)
  • PR proposing direct access to runtime-exported values to address genjsapi requirements remains under review (#2086)
entry.summary: PPX bug fixes for unused self warnings and labelled arguments in object methods merged this week

entry.title: Week 44, 2025: Tools exit code handling fixed, test suite concurrency improved, and code formatting updated
entry.content:
Summary

Ongoing work on Wasm runtime eval access, ppx_js cleanup, and Oxcaml support

Activity

  • WASM effect handler runtime error reported by Satoru Kawahara where illegal cast exceptions occur instead of expected uncaught effect exceptions in wasmofocaml 6.0.1 (#1839)

entry.title: Week 7, 2025: New CSS Font Loading API wrapper PR opened and ongoing discussion about WebAssembly development tools
entry.content:
Summary

New CSS Font Loading API wrapper PR opened with comprehensive implementation including examples

Activity

  • CSS Font Loading API wrapper implementation opened by Mesmera (WAFT) as their first contribution to the repository, featuring complete wrapper with examples and documentation (#1833)
entry.summary: New CSS Font Loading API wrapper PR opened and ongoing discussion about WebAssembly development tools

entry.title: Week 6, 2025: Major release week with version 6.0.1 bugfix release, DOM HTML compatibility improvements, and two draft WASM PRs opened
entry.content:
Summary

Release preparation activities and two major draft WASM-related PRs opened by Jérôme Vouillon for WASI runtime and effects implementation

Activity

  • WASI runtime implementation draft PR opened by Jérôme Vouillon introducing WebAssembly System Interface support with command-line flags --enable wasi and --enable exnref for compatibility with Wizard engine and wasmtime (#1831)
  • Stack Switching proposal effects draft PR opened by Jérôme Vouillon implementing effects using WebAssembly Stack Switching with Binaryen development version requirements (#1832)
  • Version 6.0.1 bugfix release published by @hhugo containing performance improvements for continuation resuming, effects dynamic switching, and various runtime fixes including Marshal.to_buffer and Unix filesystem operations
  • DOM HTML compatibility issue opened by @hhugo questioning whether Dom_html.window methods should be optdef, leading to the compatibility improvements merged this week (#1830)
entry.summary: Major release week with version 6.0.1 bugfix release, DOM HTML compatibility improvements, and two draft WASM PRs opened

entry.title: Week 5, 2025: Major test suite expansion, runtime bug fixes, and enhanced documentation for JavaScript stacktrace features.

Summary

Runtime bug fixes, test infrastructure improvements, and documentation updates with focus on debugging capabilities.

Activity

  • Runtime stability improvements with fixes for integer overflow prevention in runtime operations (053725074395851dd00669db06286e71617d3fef)
  • WASM runtime fixes including object ID refresh during unmarshalling and Windows compatibility for IO tests (e78549ae922ad762e72786da65e84841e4fdc7b6, 3c9e46f0882da8ab8d5963c8093baeb478154ead)
  • Test synchronization tooling updated to better import OCaml test cases (9d744f57ef4b735293a6656af46edba3b22c9c94)
  • Callback testing enhanced with new tests for Js.Unsafe.callback functionality (89cad068ebb19679716fb5b4cbd7a32156d1922f)

entry.title: Week 4, 2025: Major test suite synchronization and runtime improvements including float16 bigarray support and marshaling fixes

Test infrastructure overhaul with upstream synchronization and runtime stability improvements through marshaling and bounds checking fixes

  • Test suite reorganization completed major sync with upstream OCaml including effects tests restructuring, new dynarray tests, and specialized bigarray tests (#1805)
  • Runtime stability improved through marshaling object ID refresh fix (#1810) and array creation bounds checking enhancement (522972b)
  • CI infrastructure updates including Windows WASM test flake workarounds and opam package bug fixes (6f590f7, 15ff906)

WASM Windows CI improvements and test infrastructure enhancements with issue tracking

Two new PRs opened for WASM optimization and build tooling, plus merged documentation and CI improvements

  • Build tooling discussion ongoing with Sora Morimoto exploring dune-build-info for version management (#1783)
  • Documentation milestone reached with comprehensive Wasmofocaml manual merged including installation, usage, and primitive writing guides (#1771)

entry.title: Week 1, 2025: Major runtime improvements with continuation efficiency optimizations and updated ECMAScript compatibility documentation

New draft PR opened for version management simplification and continuation efficiency issue closed

entry.title: Week 52, 2024: Major effects system enhancement with double-translation feature merged, improving performance when no effect handlers are installed
entry.content:
Summary

Multiple draft PRs continue work on WebAssembly string optimization, OCaml hints consumption, and popover API implementation

Activity

  • WebAssembly string optimization continues with Jérôme Vouillon implementing JavaScript string support in the Wasm runtime (#1772) and exploring embedded Wasm code in JavaScript (#1758)
  • OCaml compiler integration work ongoing with @hhugo developing hint consumption from OCaml compiler (#1721) and physical equality optimizations (#1745)
  • Browser API expansion with Sylvain Boilard implementing the popover API for modern web features (#1734)
  • Runtime optimization experiments including @hhugo working on monomorphic MlBytes representation (#1229) and alternative jsooTop interfaces (#833)
  • WebAssembly exception handling exploration by Jérôme Vouillon with new exception handling instructions (#1759)
entry.summary: Major effects system enhancement with double-translation feature merged, improving performance when no effect handlers are installed

entry.title: Week 51, 2024: Dropped IE support enabling modern JavaScript features and improved WASM runtime with new JSDoc feature request
entry.content:
Summary

Large WASM PR opened for JavaScript string implementation with new JSDoc feature request for TypeScript integration

Activity

  • Major WASM development with Jérôme Vouillon opening comprehensive PR for OCaml string implementation using JavaScript strings, including conditional compilation, performance improvements, and compatibility features (#1772)
  • TypeScript integration request by Sora Morimoto for multi-line JSDoc comments support to enable runtime type checking with TypeScript compiler (#1777)
  • Legacy code cleanup completed with IE support removal affecting DOM event handling, XHR requests, and HTML element coercion across multiple modules
  • Testing improvements delivered through better OCaml version constraints and output file organization for cross-version compatibility
entry.summary: Dropped IE support enabling modern JavaScript features and improved WASM runtime with new JSDoc feature request

entry.title: Week 50, 2024: Dom_html API modernization, ECMAScript documentation, and global flow analysis bug fixes merged
entry.content:
Summary

Multiple bug fixes merged, CI infrastructure updated, and build system improvements delivered

Activity

  • Dom_html modernization completed by Jérôme Vouillon removing obsolete Internet Explorer compatibility code and updating event handling to current standards addressing (#1719)
  • Runtime memory leak fixed preventing channel resource accumulation in long-running JavaScript applications
  • Global flow analysis improvements with debugging capabilities added and bug resolution for optimization correctness (#1768)
  • CI infrastructure modernized updating dependency versions, latest binaryen integration (#1767), and wasmofocaml effects support
  • Documentation enhancements with new ECMAScript feature requirements documented (#1763) and minimal Node.js version clarified (#1750)
  • Build system updates including menhir extension upgrade to 3.0 for improved parser generation
entry.summary: Dom_html API modernization, ECMAScript documentation, and global flow analysis bug fixes merged

entry.title: Week 49, 2024: Major week with Wasm_of_ocaml merge completion bringing comprehensive WebAssembly compiler and runtime capabilities
entry.content:

Two experimental draft PRs opened for WebAssembly exception handling and embedded Wasm code

Activity

  • Two new draft PRs opened by Jérôme Vouillon exploring advanced WebAssembly features including experimental exception handling instructions (#1759) and embedding Wasm code inside JavaScript scripts (#1758)
  • Build dependency issue resolved involving sedlex library version compatibility reported and fixed for version 5.9.1 by Sergey Fedorov and @hhugo (#1754)
entry.summary: Major week with Wasm_of_ocaml merge completion bringing comprehensive WebAssembly compiler and runtime capabilities

entry.title: Week 48, 2024: Major WebAssembly backend merge with enhanced DOM HTML API improvements and documentation updates
entry.content:

New PR opened for physical equality hints while major WebAssembly work was merged from ocaml-wasm fork

Activity

  • New PR opened for physical equality hints by @hhugo addressing issue #1372 but currently in draft status with merge conflicts (#1745)
  • Major WebAssembly backend merge completed with massive integration from ocaml-wasm fork bringing complete WASM compilation support including 31,000+ lines of new code across 280+ files
  • Documentation requirements clarification issue opened by @hhugo requesting documentation of minimal Node.js version 16 and ECMAScript feature requirements (#1750)
  • Release 5.9 preparation merged with version bumps and CI improvements including opam constraint adjustments and macOS CI fixes
entry.summary: Major WebAssembly backend merge with enhanced DOM HTML API improvements and documentation updates

entry.title: Week 47, 2024: Release week featuring js_of_ocaml 5.9.0 with runtime improvements, JavaScript optimization fixes, and OCaml 5.3 support
entry.content:
Summary

Major release 5.9.0 published with arity propagation feature merged after 8 months, plus documentation bug fix for online toplevel usage

Activity

  • Release 5.9.0 published featuring comprehensive runtime improvements, JavaScript optimizations, and OCaml 5.3 support by @hhugo (#1735)
  • Arity propagation feature merged after 8-month development addressing function arity computation across compilation units with shape analysis and immutability tracking by @hhugo (#1594)
  • Build system enhancements including opam constraint adjustments and test infrastructure improvements for recursive value compilation and primitive checking (#1742)
entry.summary: Release week featuring js_of_ocaml 5.9.0 with runtime improvements, JavaScript optimization fixes, and OCaml 5.3 support

entry.title: Week 45, 2024: Major repository restructuring with extensive wasm_of_ocaml removal and new DOM API additions
entry.content:
Summary

Repository underwent extensive cleanup removing wasmofocaml components and modernizing structure

Activity

  • Repository restructuring involved moving JavaScript runtime files from runtime/ to runtime/js/ directory and removing extensive wasmofocaml-related code (#1727)
  • Wasmofocaml removal eliminated over 30,000 lines of WebAssembly-related code including compiler binaries, runtime files, and test infrastructure, simplifying the codebase
  • Source map improvements with code cleanup and enhanced Wasm source map handling for better debugging support
  • Documentation updates included typo fixes (#1732) and Cmdliner documentation improvements
  • CI and build system updates with workflow adjustments and dependency updates including Yojson and dune pinning
entry.summary: Major repository restructuring with extensive wasm_of_ocaml removal and new DOM API additions

entry.title: Week 42, 2024: Complete removal of wasm_of_ocaml backend (~32K lines), sourcemap improvements, and enhanced weak reference runtime
entry.content:
Summary

Major architectural shift with extensive code removal and new feature request for Dom_html modernization

Activity

  • Complete architectural transformation removing all WebAssembly compilation support in favor of pure JavaScript target, affecting compiler, runtime, and build systems (4cd7df4)
  • Documentation rewrite completely updating README from wasmofocaml focus to comprehensive jsofocaml documentation with installation guides, feature descriptions, and data representation details (4cd7df4)
  • Runtime system modernization across multiple JavaScript modules including improvements to weak references, bigarray handling, and parsing functions (4cd7df4)
  • Build system cleanup removing wasm-related build configurations, test suites, and GitHub workflow files while updating opam package specifications (4cd7df4)
entry.summary: Complete removal of wasm_of_ocaml backend (~32K lines), sourcemap improvements, and enhanced weak reference runtime
